title: Receiver General Concentrator Account Deposits
body_text:
Receiver General Concentrator Account Deposits
1	Introduction to Receiver General Concentrator Account Deposits
1.1	Overview of Receiver General Concentrator Account Deposits
Federal departments and agencies (e.g., prisons, RCMP) can request to open a Receiver General Concentrator holding account on behalf of the Receiver General of Canada with a local financial institution. Central 1 recommends that your financial institution accepts Receiver General deposits if approached. Accepting Receiver General deposits is a condition of Central 1âs participation in the Memorandum of Understanding with the Government of Canada as the group clearer for credit unions for all forms of payments.
Your financial institution uses the Receiver General Concentrator holding account to accept deposits for the Receiver General of Canada. The Receiver General Concentrator holding account acts as a pass-through account for settlement with Central 1 and the Receiver General via the Central 1 Receiver General Concentrator Account. The Central 1 Receiver General Concentrator account is in Canadian funds. Deposits are prepared using the Receiver General Account Deposit Slip, Form 6334.
On the day deposits are made, your financial institution credits the appropriate Receiver General Concentrator holding account, submits a Receiver General Deposit Request on ServiceNow, and processes cheques included in the deposit in Branch Capture. Central 1 generates a pre-authorized debit (PAD) against each account (the sweep) and credits the Receiver General. Each Receiver General Concentrator holding account should be at a zero balance once the sweep is processed.
